title: scry
description:
Searchable GraphQL ↔ MCP bridge for AI agents.

v0.1 shipped core; v0.2 hardened deployment; v0.3 closed the audit
+ caching loop; v0.4 sharpened dedupe / discoverability; v0.5
hardened the wedge (server coverage 33% → 63.8%, mutation E2E);
v0.6 built the reproducible benchmark proof artifact.

v0.7 closes the feature gap across three tiers identified in the
post-bench gap audit:

  Tier 1 (blocks adoption): field-level authz, embedding-backed
    schema_search, Docker + Helm distribution, result projection +
    pagination helpers.

  Tier 2 (friction tax): few-shot examples in tool descriptions,
    `scry init` wizard, named persisted queries, cache observability
    tools, schema-relationship graph queries.

  Tier 3 (revenue / scale unlock): multi-tenant isolation, OAuth2
    client-credentials + JWT refresh, PostgreSQL + Redis backend,
    schema_diff webhooks, usage metering primitives.

Scope is large — explicit goal is "ship every feature gap the v0.6
audit flagged" so v0.8 can shift back to bench-driven validation
+ distribution motion with a complete product surface.

features.title: deny-list per field for clients.yml
features.description:
Per-tool authz exists (admin vs read-only); per-field doesn't.
Add `deny_fields: ["User.email", "*.password"]` to clients.yml
identity entries. query_validate + query_execute reject any
query touching a denied field with a permission_denied envelope
carrying the violating selection set. Pattern matching: exact
"Type.field" + glob "*.field" (any type) + glob "Type.*" (any
field).

Audit chain Evidence carries the deny match. Tests cover: no
rules (passthrough), one exact rule, glob match, transitive
access via fragment / inline-fragment, alias rename doesn't
bypass.

features.title: hybrid BM25 + vector schema_search
features.description:
Replace pure BM25 with hybrid score: α·BM25 + (1−α)·cosine
similarity against type/field embedded descriptions. Agent
paraphrases ("customer contact info" → email) now hit when they
previously missed.

sqlite-vec extension (modernc.org/sqlite supports loadable
extensions). Embeddings via configurable provider — default to
a tiny on-disk model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 via onnxruntime-go) so
ops doesn't need a separate embedding service. Falls back to
pure BM25 when extension or model unavailable.

Bench impact: feed v0.6 results back — embedding adoption gated
on bench showing pure BM25 misses on real schemas.

features.title: query_execute select + paginate helpers
features.description:
query_execute returns full upstream response. Common waste:
`pullRequests(first:100) { nodes { …200 fields } }` blows the
agent's context whether it needs every field or not.

Add two optional arguments:
  - select: JMESPath expression projected against the response
    body before return. Errors → invalid_select envelope.
  - paginate: { auto: bool, max_pages: int }. When true scry
    follows pageInfo.hasNextPage cursors automatically up to
    max_pages, concatenating nodes[].

Audit chain records selection projection + pagination depth so
operator can see post-filter cost reduction.

features.title: published Docker image + Helm chart + k8s manifests
features.description:
Binary covers desktop MCP clients; hosted scry needs k8s. Ship:
  - multi-arch ghcr.io image via goreleaser (linux/amd64,
    linux/arm64)
  - charts/scry/ Helm chart (Service, Deployment, ConfigMap for
    servers.yml + clients.yml, Secret for tokens, optional
    PodMonitor for Prometheus)
  - deploy/k8s/ raw manifests for users who don't use Helm
  - GitHub Actions workflow that publishes the image on tag
    push and pushes the chart to charts.yml on the gh-pages
    branch

Image runs as non-root, includes a HEALTHCHECK, exposes
/healthz on the http transport. Existing Dockerfile gets a
polish pass.

features.title: few-shot examples in tool descriptions
features.description:
Tool descriptions are prose. Anthropic + OpenAI docs both
recommend 1-2 concrete examples in tool descriptions to help
models pick the right tool. scry has zero.

Add an `examples` array to each tool registration. Renderer
prepends them to the existing prose. Examples cover: the
happy path + the gotcha (e.g. schema_search empty-result hint;
query_execute hash-vs-query mutual exclusion).

No public API change — tool descriptions are strings that mcp-
go renders.

features.title: scry init wizard
features.description:
Operator first-run = read README, edit servers.yml, hope.
`scry init [--upstream URL] [--token TOK]` prompts for
upstream, runs an introspection probe, writes
$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/scry/servers.yml with mode 0600, optionally
installs a sample claude_desktop_config.json snippet. Idempotent
— re-running detects existing server entries and offers to
replace / add / cancel.

Non-interactive mode (--upstream + --token + --yes) for
scripted setup.

features.title: persisted queries by name (not just hash)
features.description:
query_execute(hash="abc123") forces the agent to memorize hashes.
Allow optional `name` field at pq creation time; the agent can
call query_execute(name="getCustomerPRs") and scry resolves
name → hash internally.

Backward compatible: hash-only PQs still work; named PQs adopt
an extra column in the per-server pq.db. CLI: scry pq add
--name <name> --query <query>; scry pq list shows name +
truncated hash.

features.title: cache_stats + cache_purge MCP tools
features.description:
Cache hit/miss counters exist in metrics but no MCP-surface for
operators. Two new tools:

  - cache_stats() → JSON envelope of per-server hit_count,
    miss_count, eviction_count, size_entries, oldest_entry_age.
  - cache_purge(server?) → invalidates one or all caches without
    restart. permission_denied for read-only clients.

Both gated by per-tool authz (admin only by default).

features.title: schema_neighbors graph navigation tool
features.description:
"What types reference Customer?" is the agent's most common
navigation question after schema_search. Today: search +
schema_get loops. New tool: schema_neighbors(name) returns
incoming references (fields that return this type) + outgoing
references (types this type's fields return), as a JSON edge
list. Bound by neighbor depth=1 to keep payloads small.

Computed once on introspection; cached in the SQLite index as
a graph adjacency table.

features.title: per-tenant isolation
features.description:
clients.yml currently scopes one org. Add `tenant` field to
identity entries; per-tenant servers.yml override at
$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/scry/tenants/<tenant>.yml; per-tenant audit
directory + gate session-id namespacing.

The http transport reads tenant from a configurable header
(default: X-Scry-Tenant); stdio remains single-tenant. List_servers
and schema_* respect tenant scoping — an agent in tenant A
never sees servers/data from tenant B.

features.title: OAuth2 client-credentials + JWT refresh
features.description:
Today: static bearer or mTLS. Most real upstreams (Salesforce,
Atlassian, Microsoft Graph) rotate via OAuth2 client-credentials
with short-lived JWTs. Add a new auth type "oauth2" to
servers.yml:

  auth:
    type: oauth2
    token_url: https://upstream/oauth/token
    client_id: env://X_CLIENT_ID
    client_secret: env://X_CLIENT_SECRET
    scopes: [read:graphql]
    refresh_skew: 60s   # refresh this many seconds before exp

Background refresher kicks off N seconds before expiry; in-flight
requests during rotation queue or use the previous token until
the new one is ready (no auth_expired storm).

features.title: PostgreSQL index + Redis cache for HA
features.description:
SQLite is right for one process. Multi-instance HA needs shared
state. Add optional drivers:

  - postgres for the schema index (replace store.go's sqlite
    path behind a Store interface) + audit chain replay
  - redis for the result cache (replace cache.Cache with a
    backend interface)

Selected via servers.yml top-level `storage:` block; defaults
stay sqlite + in-memory so existing deployments don't change.

features.title: schema_diff_subscribe + outbound webhook
features.description:
Today: agents see diffs only via schema_diff polling. Add
schema_diff_subscribe(server, webhook_url) — registers a
webhook fired with the diff body on every successful refresh
that produces a non-empty diff. Persisted in
<indexdir>/<server>.webhooks.db. CLI: scry webhooks list /
remove. HMAC-signed payloads keyed off a per-webhook secret.

features.title: usage_stats per session + per tenant
features.description:
Audit chain hashes everything but isn't aggregated for billing.
Add a metering layer that accumulates per-session + per-tenant:
  - tool_calls_total{tool, outcome}
  - upstream_bytes_in / upstream_bytes_out
  - upstream_latency_ms (avg + p95)
  - complexity_consumed
  - dollars_consumed (configurable per-tool cost table)

Exposed via two surfaces: usage_stats() MCP tool (operator-only)
+ Prometheus /metrics endpoint with the same numbers.

features.title: v0.7 CHANGELOG + tag + release notes
features.description:
Final step: CHANGELOG sweep covering all 14 feature deliveries,
tag v0.7.0, ensure goreleaser publishes binaries + Docker image
+ Helm chart in lockstep.
